subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Calculate the Arithmetic Mean of a dataset
Construct Bar Graphs with appropriate scales
Interpret data trends from visual representations

lesson_plan.title: Mean, Range, and Mode
topics_covered: Finding the spread of data using Range
teaching_strategy: Example of mode in real life (most popular shoe size)
[Image showing calculation of mean, median, and mode for a data set]
student_activities: Calculate the mean height of a group of 5 students
Find the range and mode of a set of test marks
assessment: Exit slip: 'Can a dataset have two modes?'

lesson_plan.title: Constructing Bar Graphs
topics_covered: Choosing a suitable scale for the Y-axis
Drawing bars of uniform width and spacing
Labeling axes and giving a title
teaching_strategy: Discussion on how scale choice affects the look of the graph
student_activities: Draw a bar graph showing 'Hours of TV watched' by different students
Re-draw a graph using a different scale to see the visual change
assessment: Bar graph neatness and accuracy rubric

teacher_tip: If a bar graph looks 'too tall' or 'too squashed,' the scale needs to be adjusted. Remind students to pick a scale that uses at least half of the graph paper.
